title: fixed options not having headless attribute
body:
Fixes errors:

line 398, in __init__
    if headless or options.headless:
                   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'ChromeOptions' object has no attribute 'headless'


line 488, in __init__
    if options.headless:
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'ChromeOptions' object has no attribute 'headless'

step_plan.summary: Fix `AttributeError` crashes by ensuring headless state is safely accessible even when Selenium options objects don’t expose a `.headless` attribute. Update `Chrome.__init__` to guard reads and update `ChromeOptions.from_options` to guarantee a default `headless` attribute after cloning.

steps.operation: Make headless checks robust by using a safe accessor and persisting a default headless flag on the options object
steps.reason: `Chrome.__init__` currently reads `options.headless` directly (e.g., `if headless or options.headless:` and later `if options.headless:`), but Selenium’s `ChromeOptions/ChromiumOptions` may not define a `.headless` attribute, causing `AttributeError`. Using `getattr(options, 'headless', False)` (or equivalent) eliminates the crash and allows both UC options and Selenium-native options to work. Persisting the resolved value via `setattr(options, 'headless', bool_value)` ensures later code paths have a consistent attribute to read.
steps.side_effects: May slightly change behavior for callers passing a Selenium options instance that happens to have a different headless representation (arguments only): the code will now infer headless from the explicit `headless` parameter and/or existing headless args if you add that inference. Ensure downstream logic that injects/removes `--headless*` flags still behaves as expected across Chrome versions; run any integration tests that launch Chrome in headless/non-headless modes.

steps.operation: Ensure cloned/normalized options always have a `headless` attribute with a safe default after copying from external options
steps.reason: `ChromeOptions.from_options` clones an arbitrary Selenium options object (often via `__dict__`). If the source options lacks `.headless`, the resulting UC `ChromeOptions` can also lack it, and later reads in `Chrome.__init__` (or other code) can fail. After cloning, explicitly set `headless` to `getattr(source, 'headless', False)` (or default `False`) on the returned `ChromeOptions` instance so the attribute is always present and predictable.
steps.side_effects: If callers rely on headless being implied solely by presence of `--headless*` arguments, you may want to keep `headless` defaulting to `False` unless explicitly set; otherwise you could optionally infer from arguments. Validate that `from_options()` continues to preserve other option fields (binary location, prefs, experimental options) and doesn’t regress serialization or equality expectations.
